element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Standard movement, shooting, jumping mechanics establish baseline
Auto-aiming guns, Barn Blaster (clears all visible enemies), expanded arsenal beyond original
Can stick to slimy surfaces, swing from platforms, function as parachute on jumps—enables new traversal paths
Increases suit power to 200%, enables enhanced abilities
Cave salamander disguise in intestinal passages, exploding sheep hazards, game show trivia section—major shift from platforming
Jim's head inflates; player controls upward float, avoids sharp objects (fall triggers), dodges Evil the Cat—vertical momentum puzzle
Pocket rocket vehicle, balloon/bomb direction control, defend from enemies, defeat Major Mucus—radically different paradigm

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Final Level: See Jim Run, Run Jim Run
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Race vs. Psy-Crow through obstacle course instead of traditional boss fight—climactic variant
Mentioned in manual but reportedly absent from US SNES release; grayscale/colorful palette inversion—cosmetic/exploration reward

typical_relief_triggers: Completion of non-standard level variant (trivia section ends, balloon phase ends)
Obtaining power-ups or new gun types
Safe platform sections between hazard gauntlets
Snott grapple/swing sections allowing momentum recovery
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new game mechanic mid-level (Villi People trivia shift)
Hazard-dense level sections (exploding sheep, sharp objects, enemy spawns)
Mechanic-specific failure states (balloon pops, isometric movement miscues)
Psy-Crow final race against time pressure
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Highly varied; alternates standard platforming with abrupt genre shifts and puzzle-like challenges; maintains unpredictability and novelty across 9+ levels

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden room mentioned in Level Ate (referenced in cheat menu tips); exploration-based secret discovery
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Collecting TV items reveals hidden color palettes (mentioned in tips: 'COLLECTING THE COLOUR T.V'S WILL GIVE YOU REALLY STRANGE COLORED LEVELS'); Snott-based traversal unlocks alternative paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible run-and-gun platforming foundation (Anything but Tangerines); introduces expanded move set and weapon variety without overwhelming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Genre-shifting challenges intensify (Villi People's salamander disguise + trivia, Inflated Head's float control, The Flyin' King's isometric shooter); players must rapidly adapt to new mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: Climactic race scenario (See Jim Run, Run Jim Run) combines learned mechanics under time pressure; narrative subversion (Princess/Psy-Crow revealed as cows) provides tonal shift rather than escalating difficulty
notable_spikes: Level 3 (Villi People): Abrupt shift to cave traversal + trivia format; no logical correct answers create cognitive dissonance spike
Inflated Head: Physics-based challenge (balloon float + Evil the Cat) contrasts sharply with prior platforming rhythms
The Flyin' King: Isometric perspective and vehicle control introduce entirely new motor-skill demands
Final level: Time-pressure element on obstacle course adds urgency atypical of prior stages

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels (Anything but Tangerines, Level Ate) maintain run-and-gun/platformer structure but expand arsenal, movement options, and enemy variety relative to original game

Mid-level sections introduce entirely different gameplay paradigms: Villi People combines exploration with game-show trivia; Inflated Head adds physics-puzzle float mechanics; The Flyin' King switches to isometric shooter—each level teaches new rules mid-play
Levels emphasize environmental hazards over enemy combat; exploding sheep, sharp objects, intestinal walls require timing and spatial awareness rather than gun-focus
Levels lock Jim into alternate form (salamander, balloon, rocket) that completely redefines input mapping and spatial navigation; requires learning new control paradigm per level
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Inflated Head, The Flyin' King, Villi People

notable_level_patterns.description: Final level (See Jim Run, Run Jim Run) is structured as race rather than boss fight; combines learned elements under time pressure; ends in meta twist (all characters revealed as cows)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: See Jim Run, Run Jim Run

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor: Earthworm Jim 2 SNES)
